If you die at night, you are woken to choose a player: you learn their character.
Each night, you learn how many of your 2 alive neighbours are evil.

Each night*, you learn which character the player that was executed today is.
Each night*, choose a player: they die. If you kill yourself this way, a Minion becomes the Imp.
Each night*, choose a player (not yourself): they are safe from the Demon tonight.

data.content: Professor, your lies are as transparent as the ghosts among us—I stood before this town and declared myself the Monk, yet you try to rewrite the very words I spoke! Your desperation to frame Twill only confirms what her vision told us: you are the murderer, and today you will pay for it.

You have died. Your role remains hidden from other players. You continue to participate fully in discussions and breakout groups. You cannot nominate. You have exactly 1 ghost vote remaining for the rest of the game — once you use it, you cannot vote again. You no longer receive night ability information.
You learn that Twill [Seat 5] is the Imp.
A haunted ottoman finally achieved its lifelong dream of revenge, flattening Peppercorn like a particularly seasoned crepe.

You have died. Your role remains hidden from other players. You continue to participate fully in discussions and breakout groups. You cannot nominate. You have exactly 1 ghost vote remaining for the rest of the game — once you use it, you cannot vote again. You no longer receive night ability information.

Oh come *on*! I had the Slayer the whole time and never got to use it?! And Twill was the Imp all along—I should've pushed harder on them instead of getting distracted. Well played, evil team, but that one stings.
I knew Twill was being a bit too quiet, but I never expected them to be the Imp! Hazelnut, you had the shot right there—well played to the evil team for pulling that off.
Oof, I was the Empath and still got dragged to the graveyard before I could make it matter. Nice work to Twill and the Scarlet Woman though — classic evil grind, very clean finish.
Wow, I can't believe we pulled it off! That last minute bluffing really paid off, and Twill played an amazing Imp. Hats off to the good team for keeping us on our toes the whole game!

Well, *that* explains everything! I was trying to piece together the dead townsfolk like some kind of detective, but apparently Twill had me fooled the whole time. Respect to Evil for playing it clean—at least I went out doing my job properly, even if it didn't matter in the end.

I knew keeping Abbot Hazelnut alive was a massive risk—I can't believe I dodged a Slayer shot to the very end! Saffron, you were a legendary Scarlet Woman, thanks for holding the line while I caused chaos. What a win!

I knew I should’ve been more suspicious of Twill! I spent the whole game protecting the wrong people while the Imp was hiding right in plain sight. Well played, evil team—you really had us chasing our tails.

players.ability_text: Once per game, during the day, publicly choose a player: if they are the Demon, they die.
If you die at night, you are woken to choose a player: you learn their character.
Each night, you learn how many of your 2 alive neighbours are evil.
If there are 5 or more players alive & the Demon dies, you become the Demon.
Each night*, you learn which character the player that was executed today is.
Each night*, choose a player: they die. If you kill yourself this way, a Minion becomes the Imp.
Each night*, choose a player (not yourself): they are safe from the Demon tonight.
